title: Gut Microbiome Analysis in Mice
author: Trina Knotts
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ykjfuun

steps.title: Mice are singly housed in clean...
steps.instruction: Mice are singly housed in clean cages with fresh food and water and low bedding.

steps.title: Weigh mouse and “food-in” (grams of...
steps.instruction: Weigh mouse and “food-in” (grams of rodent diet provided at start of experiment).

steps.title: Save several pellets of rodent diet for analysis (e
steps.instruction: Save several pellets of rodent diet for analysis (e.g., for energy or macronutrient content)-Store at 4ºC in airtight container.

steps.title: At 24h, weigh mouse and “food...
steps.instruction: At 24h, weigh mouse and “food –out” (grams of rodent diet remaining)

steps.title: At the end of 48h collection...
steps.instruction: At the end of 48h collection period, weigh mouse and “food-out”.

steps.title: Swap out the cage bottoms with...
steps.instruction: Swap out the cage bottoms with new clean bottoms, replace feeder insert/water bottle and returnanimals to housing system.

steps.title: For each cage, carefully inspect and...
steps.instruction: For each cage, carefully inspect and remove each piece of bedding using tweezers to collect allfeces. Inspect each pellet of feces to assure that there is no spilled food or fibers of hair or bedding attached. If significant food spillage is found in cage bottom, weigh in pre-tared weigh boat to add back to food measurement.

steps.title: Place feces in labeled pre-weighed 1
steps.instruction: Place feces in labeled pre-weighed 1.5 ml tube.

steps.title: Subtract this value from original tube...
steps.instruction: Subtract this value from original tube weight to determine fecal “wet” weight.

steps.title: After collection, fecal samples are stored at -80C until analysis
steps.instruction: After collection, fecal samples are stored at -80C until analysis.

steps.title: Samples will be shipped/delivered on dry...
steps.instruction: Samples will be shipped/delivered on dry ice to UCDavis MBP to coordinate 16S DNA ampliconsequencing and analysis. Samples should be delivered/shipped to: Mouse Biology ProgramC/O UC Davis MMPC University of California2795 Second Street, Suite 400 Davis, CA 95618 530-757-3333 Please include MBP/MMPC project # in reference section on package documentation and send the FedEx/UPS tracking number information to : mmpc-bodyc@ucdavis.edu Note: It is not critical to perform food intake measurements during this collection time but it is beneficial for analysis/QC.

steps.title: Data collected
steps.instruction: Data collected: Microbial community structure (differences in gut microbe population prevalence) Correlation of results with metabolic phenotype/diet variables
